scope: sworn regress runs the Go test suite from the directory containing go.mod (root or a first-level subdirectory), not always the worktree root, so a repo whose Go module lives in a subdirectory (e.g. fired's <repo>/go) reports real Go test results instead of a spurious 'directory prefix . does not contain main module' setup failure.

reachability.evidence: TestRunRegress_GoModuleInSubdir (internal/gate/regress_test.go) drives the same runRegress entry point the sworn regress CLI calls, against a fixture with go.mod at <worktree>/go: the mock only satisfies the 'go' test './...' key when dir == the module subdir, so the test would fail with ExitCode -1 (unmocked key) if runGoSuite still dispatched at the worktree root. It asserts Passed=true and Skipped=false, i.e. the Go suite executed from the module directory, reproducing the fired-shaped defect fix end-to-end through the CLI-invoked gate function.

delivered.item: findGoModuleDir discovers the Go module dir: worktree root first, else exactly one first-level subdirectory (skipping .git/vendor/node_modules/testdata and other hidden dirs).
AC-01: runGoSuite runs go test ./... with cmd.Dir = the discovered module dir, not the worktree root.
AC-02: root-module case (sworn's own shape) continues to pass; go test ./internal/gate/... passes.
delivered.evidence: TestRunRegress_AllPass, TestRunRegress_AllFail, TestRunRegress_Mixed, TestRunRegress_NoPackageJSON (all now carry a root go.mod fixture); go test ./internal/gate/... exit 0

delivered.item: AC-03: no go.mod anywhere under the worktree -> Go suite reported Skipped with a scan-bound-honest reason, not a hard FAIL.
delivered.evidence: TestRunRegress_NoGoMod; skip reason 'no go.mod at worktree root or in a first-level subdirectory' (design-review pin 1 applied)

delivered.item: D1: multiple first-level Go modules -> skipped with a distinct multi-module reason, not an arbitrary pick.
D1/D2 recorded as Type-2 noted defaults in status.json per Coach pin disposition.
Full-suite regression sweep (pin 3) confirms no cross-package fixture breakage, including cmd/sworn's real-RunRegress integration tests tolerating the FAIL->Skipped flip.
delivered.evidence: go test -timeout 300s ./... — all packages ok, including cmd/sworn
